summary: PG bridge files (.pg.sql.ts) re-export sqliteTable() definitions — cannot safely generate PG DDL from these schemas
details: All .pg.sql.ts files import from drizzle-orm/sqlite-core and re-export sqliteTable() definitions as "PG" schemas. schema.pg.sql.ts explicitly imports `integer` from sqlite-core. Running drizzle-kit with dialect: "postgresql" against these will generate wrong column types (no jsonb, boolean, etc.). The existing migration-pg/ DDL was generated one-off and uses native PG types. Fix: rewrite .pg.sql.ts files to use pgTable() from drizzle-orm/pg-core with PG-native column types. This blocks safe use of db:generate:pg.

summary: PGlite migration path in applyMigrations() doesn't wrap SQL statements in a transaction — partial failure corrupts schema
details: The PGlite branch of applyMigrations() (db.pg.ts:31-37) executes SQL statements in a bare for loop with no BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK. If statement N of 3 fails, statements 1..N-1 are committed — schema is partially applied. The node-postgres branch correctly uses drizzle-orm's migrate() which wraps in a transaction. Fix: wrap the loop in client.exec('BEGIN') / COMMIT / ROLLBACK.
